helpers.name: add_module

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Directory path relative to source root (e.g., 'src/wallet_connection/')

parameters.name: purpose
parameters.description: Domain concern this module owns (one sentence)

helpers.purpose: Create a new module (reusable code boundary). A module maps to a directory. Files are assigned explicitly via add_file_to_module. Use external_dependencies to record wrapped libraries.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error on duplicate name or path
return_statements: Module created. Now write files under this module's path and assign them via add_file_to_module(file_id, module_id) after finalize_file.
If this module wraps external libraries: all project code should import from this module, never from the library directly.

used_by_directives.description: Create module entry when AI determines code should be organized as a reusable module
Create module if file_write determines new domain logic needs module organization

helpers.name: get_module_by_name
helpers.purpose: Get module by name. Returns module record with path, purpose, and external_dependencies.

used_by_directives.description: Check if a module already exists before creating a new one

helpers.name: get_all_modules
helpers.purpose: Get all modules in the project. Returns list of all module records sorted by name.
helpers.error_handling: Returns empty tuple if no modules exist
return_statements: Review modules list to determine if new code fits an existing module before creating a new one.
If modules list is empty and project has domain logic: consider whether modules should be created via project_module_check.

used_by_directives.description: Review all existing modules to determine if new code fits an existing module
Include module count and list in project status overview

helpers.name: update_module
helpers.purpose: Update module metadata. Only non-None fields are updated.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found or integrity violation

helpers.name: delete_module
helpers.purpose: Delete a module. CASCADE removes module_files junction entries. Files themselves are NOT deleted.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found. Creates audit note on deletion.

helpers.name: add_file_to_module
helpers.purpose: Assign a file to a module. Creates module_files junction entry. Same pattern as add_file_to_flow.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if file or module not found, or if already assigned
return_statements: File assigned to module. Proceed with function/type finalization and interaction tracking as normal.
If this file imports external libraries that the module wraps: verify the module's external_dependencies field includes them.

used_by_directives.description: After finalizing a file, assign it to its module
Assign written file to its module after finalization

helpers.name: remove_file_from_module
helpers.purpose: Remove a file from a module. Deletes module_files junction entry. File itself is not deleted.

helpers.name: get_module_files
helpers.purpose: Get all files assigned to a module via module_files junction table.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found

used_by_directives.description: Inspect what files already exist in a module before adding new ones

helpers.name: get_module_functions
helpers.purpose: Get all functions in module files. Joins through module_files junction to files to functions.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found

helpers.name: get_module_types
helpers.purpose: Get all types in module files. Joins through module_files junction to files to types.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found

helpers.name: get_module_dependencies
helpers.purpose: Get cross-module dependencies. Finds interactions where a function in this module calls a function in a different module via module_files junction. Returns aggregated counts per target module.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if module not found

helpers.name: get_module_for_file
helpers.purpose: Find which module a file belongs to. Reverse lookup via module_files junction table.
helpers.error_handling: Returns None if file is not in any module. Returns error if file not found.
return_statements: If module is None: this file is not assigned to any module. Evaluate whether it should be via project_module_check — domain logic files should always belong to a module.
If module found: file is tracked. Proceed with normal workflow.

used_by_directives.description: Check if file being written belongs to a module for proper organization
After reserving a file, check if it should be assigned to a module

helpers.name: get_unassigned_files
helpers.purpose: Find files not assigned to any module. Useful for identifying tracking gaps — domain logic files that should belong to a module but haven't been assigned.
helpers.error_handling: Returns empty tuple if all files are assigned
return_statements: Review unassigned files: orchestrators (pages, handlers, commands, configs) are expected to be unassigned. Domain logic files should be assigned to modules via add_file_to_module.

used_by_directives.description: Report files not assigned to any module as potential tracking gaps

helpers.name: search_modules
helpers.purpose: Search modules by name, purpose, or description. Uses FTS5 ranked search with LIKE fallback.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if search string empty
return_statements: If matches found: review module purpose and external_dependencies to determine if your code belongs in an existing module rather than creating a new one.
If no matches: consider whether a new module is needed via project_module_check.

used_by_directives.description: Search for existing modules that might already cover the domain logic being written
Search for modules during file_write module gate

helpers.name: add_files_to_module

parameters.name: links
parameters.description: List of [file_id, module_id] tuples to assign

helpers.purpose: Batch assign multiple files to modules. More efficient than multiple add_file_to_module calls. Skips duplicates silently, fails fast on missing entities.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error on empty list or missing file/module IDs. Skips existing assignments with skipped_count.
return_statements: Files assigned to modules. Proceed with function/type finalization and interaction tracking.
If any files import external libraries that the module wraps: verify the module's external_dependencies field includes them.

used_by_directives.description: After finalizing multiple files, batch assign them to their modules

helpers.name: remove_files_from_module

parameters.name: links
parameters.description: List of [file_id, module_id] tuples to remove

helpers.purpose: Batch remove multiple files from modules. More efficient than multiple remove_file_from_module calls. Skips non-existent entries, reports modules left empty.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error on empty list or missing module IDs. Reports emptied modules in empty_modules field.
return_statements: If empty_modules is non-empty: consider deleting empty modules via delete_module or reassigning files.
